summary: Skill Advisor discovery is skill-first: the compatibility path discovers top-level SKILL.md files and builds records with kind/source set to skill, so .opencode/agents/multi-ai-council.md is outside the normal advisor corpus.

The explicit scorer maps tokens and phrases to skill IDs and skill command aliases; there are no current multi-ai-council or council boosts, and a live council prompt returned no advisor recommendation at threshold 0.8.
evidence: python3 .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/skill_advisor/scripts/skill_advisor.py "Use multi-ai-council to produce a planning council for this architecture decision" --threshold 0.8 -> []

summary: Direct routing already exists in orchestrate.md: multi-strategy planning and architecture synthesis maps to @multi-ai-council, and the agent is explicitly LEAF.
As long as ADR-001 keeps multi-ai-council out of .opencode/skills/, Skill Advisor should not return it as a pseudo-skill recommendation.

Council implementation prompts can still route through Skill Advisor to sk-code/system-spec-kit when the requested action is code or spec work; the abstention applies to running the planning agent itself.
Do not add TOKEN_BOOSTS.council or PHRASE_BOOSTS['multi-ai-council'] unless the advisor gains a first-class agent recommendation surface.
If mirror-sync automation adds generic agent metadata, revisit whether that metadata should remain validation-only or become advisor-visible as a separate agent lane.
